source.definition: characterized by complete disorder and confusion; lacking organization or order

explanation.sourceText: The adjective "chaotic" derives from the French word "chaotique," which ultimately comes from the Greek "chaos," meaning a state of complete disorder or confusion.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „chaotic” provine din francezul „chaotique”, care la rândul său derivă din grecescul „chaos”, semnificând o stare de dezordine completă sau confuzie.

source.additional: Chaotic situations can often be overwhelming and usually require special measures to resolve.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "haotic" is borrowed from the French "chaotique," which in turn comes from the Greek "chaos."
explanation.targetText: Termenul românesc „haotic” provine din franceză „chaotique”, derivat din grecescul „chaos”.
Se referă la lipsa de ordine sau confuzie.

examples.sourceText: The chaotic situation was confusing for everyone.
examples.targetText: Situația haotică a fost confuză pentru toți.

examples.sourceText: The chaotic conditions made it difficult to develop a clear plan.
examples.targetText: Condițiile haotice au făcut dificilă dezvoltarea unui plan clar.

examples.sourceText: In chaotic education, it is hard to learn effectively.
examples.targetText: În învățământul haotic este greu să înveți eficient.

examples.sourceText: The room always looks chaotic when he leaves it.
examples.targetText: Camera arată întotdeauna haotic când el o părăsește.

examples.sourceText: The chaotic situation requires immediate measures.
He always brings chaotic ideas to meetings.
examples.targetText: El întotdeauna aduce idei haotice în întâlniri.

learningTips.text: Think of a chaotic classroom where papers and books are everywhere — it helps to remember the word!
Imagine a chaotic traffic jam with cars honking and no order to recall the meaning of "haotic."
Link "chaotic" with the English word "chaos," which sounds similar and means disorder.
Remember the Romanian word "haotic" by associating it with the English word "chaotic" — they share the same root and meaning.
